title: Test Add collapsible sections in Details panel

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`, `tests/test_tui.py`, and the README guidance for collapsible detail sections. Confirmed dedicated regression coverage for collapsed-by-default sections, keyboard section navigation/toggling, header-click expansion, and adjacent help/scroll behavior. Ran `python -m unittest tests.test_tui.TuiRegressionTests.test_detail_panel_uses_collapsible_sections_with_compact_defaults tests.test_tui.TuiRegressionTests.test_detail_section_header_click_toggles_collapsible tests.test_tui.TuiRegressionTests.test_help_overlay_text_documents_toggle_shortcuts`, `python -m unittest tests.test_tui.TuiRegressionTests.test_detail_scroll_reuses_rendered_content_during_keyboard_scroll tests.test_tui.TuiRegressionTests.test_help_overlay_close_preserves_pending_merge_until_confirmed_after_close`, and `python -m unittest tests.test_tui`; all passed.
handoff_summary.remaining: No tester-scope work remains for this bead.
handoff_summary.risks: Validation used `python -m unittest` because `pytest` is not installed in the current environment. No unresolved behavioral failures were observed in the exercised TUI paths.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: This bead touches shared TUI detail-panel behavior, regression coverage, and shortcut/docs wording. Downstream agents editing the same panel interactions or operator shortcut text should rebase carefully on README.md, src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py, and tests/test_tui.py.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run targeted checks and summarize failures clearly.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes only when strictly necessary to execute or stabilize the tests.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.
